name: Caldas (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the wealth of Caldas, including coffee, gold and other natural riches
the fertile mountains, forests and coffee plantations of the department

description: A vertical bicolour of yellow at the hoist and green at the fly, in equal halves.
history: Adopted as the flag of the Department of Caldas, created in 1905 from parts of Antioquia and Cauca. The yellow and green colours evoke the gold and the coffee-growing landscape that have defined the region's economy.

features.symbolism: yellow for the wealth of the territory, especially its coffee and gold, and green for the fertile mountains and forests
